唔係只係受害人可以投訴該護士，任何人都可以

據消息人士透露，如果有具體地點，時間，都可投訴，要求 qeh 交名，如不交，可以要求 nursing council 引用有關法例交名，而且 a&e 外面應該可能有 cctv ，或是（未試過唔知得唔得）可要求交出 cctv 記錄。

去 nursing council 投訴，最好揾律師出投訴信及覆，因為一般小市民， nursing council 一般會耍，用法例耍，扮有理據唔處理。

a practice on writing HK issue in English
please give comments if you want

________________________________

Medical staff working in the public health sector in Hong Kong wrote an open letter to the chief executive of the Hospital Authority, the managing body of public hospitals, according to HA Secrets, a Facebook page where staff of the Hospital Authority reportedly become whistleblowers .

They questioned whether the chief executive was being misled, or deliberately shielded his staff.

The writers claimed that the police officer present in the accident and emergency department on the day of the arrest of the protester walked around the area. When the protester registered, the officer reportedly asked how he injured his head and where he did it. The protester was said to stay silent.

Then the officer allegedly asked, “will you not know where you injured yourself?”
The officer reportedly knocked on the window of the triage station, and asked for a label (that contained the personal information of the patients).

Then apparently the protester was not accompanied by police officers. However, the officer was said to have been looking for him, but in vain. Later, some police officers in black clothing conducted an arrest, according to the post.

The person who leaked the info was said to be a registered nurse who was awaiting promotion and was studying. He/she was said to have given the card to the police because of immense pressure. The management reportedly forgave her, because she was not to repeat the same mistake again.

The whole witness process seemed to have been seen by one staff only. The writers hoped that the Hospital Authority could find this witness, and complain to the Nursing Council.
The senior management of the Queen Elizabeth Hospital, according to this post, wanted to “protect” the nurse concerned, and so the issue was elevated to the publication in newspaper in the first instance already.

The post then went on to say that now that information was available, it was hoped that the investigation results would lead to disciplinary actions. It did not want to reveal the name of the nurse now.
